name: Self-Assembled Monolayers (SAMs)
description: Self-assembled monolayers form when amphiphilic molecules chemisorb through their headgroups onto a surface (e.g. thiols on gold) and then order via tail-tail van der Waals packing; coverage builds with time and annealing removes defects to yield a functional monolayer.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Substrate + amphiphile"]
    N2["Headgroup binds surface"]
    N3["Chemisorption (thiol–Au)"]
    N4["Molecules pack / order"]
    N5{"Coverage high?"}
    N6["More adsorption time"]
    N7["Tail packing (vdW)"]
    N8["Ordered monolayer"]
    N9["Anneal out defects"]
    N10["Functional SAM"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|no| N6
    N6 -->|continue| N2
    N5 -->|yes| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Adsorption of bifunctional organic disulfides on gold surfaces

sources.title: Self-assembled monolayers of thiolates on metals as a form of nanotechnology
sources.authors: Love, J. C.; Estroff, L. A.; Kriebel, J. K.; Nuzzo, R. G.; Whitesides, G. M.

sources.title: Formation and structure of self-assembled monolayers
